key_points: Redstone and Aspen Ridge dominate off-campus housing and are widely hated
Helaman Halls are elevator-free; Heritage strongly preferred over Helaman
Provo's 3-unrelated-persons zoning law traps students in limited options
key_quotes: Unfortunately they own/run 98.6% of the properties around BYU. It's nearly impossible to avoid them.
I stayed in Helaman for a week and then moved to Heritage. I could not stay there mainly because of the shower/bathroom and kitchen situation.
I wrote several negative reviews about two Redstone properties on Google and they've had them erased.

key_points: Social scene is heavily tied to LDS church wards and activities
Early marriage culture is real — divorces during undergrad are common too
Non-LDS students can participate but may feel excluded from core social circles
key_quotes: A lot of the social life can seem to be tied to the church and different social activities that are organized through the ward.
I married as an undergrad and it's been 20 years now. Best decision I ever made. But I definitely missed out on the social aspect of college after that.
Nobody here actually thinks that soaking is a loophole.

key_points: Taco Bell replaced by Costa Vida — students furious about $11 minimum price point
Open Door meal plan has confusing scanning issues with to-go boxes
Campus Creamery is beloved; soda refill crackdown caused campus-wide drama
key_quotes: I'm actually so upset about this — replacing the quintessential college student meal option with an $11 minimum restaurant.
Costa Vida is awful. I couldn't believe it when I wanted to try their raspberry chipotle chicken and it was just a cold sauce cup dumped on regular chicken.
I lived on 79-cent Cheesy Bean and Rice Burritos back in the day. RIP.

key_points: Being LGBTQ+ at BYU can cause serious mental health crises; one student withdrew entirely
Being Black or a minority can feel isolating; culture shock is explicitly warned about
Academic pressure after transfer can be crushing — one 4.0 student now barely passing
key_quotes: I would focus on how being in an overwhelmingly Mormon environment exacerbated the situation.
As a Black girl, it's been very uncomfortable for me. I'm grateful that we are safe, but right now, I don't feel comfortable going out.
I transferred to BYU and ever since I have never been so academically challenged in my life. And not the good kind of challenge, but the challenge that makes me want to drop out entirely.

key_points: Tuition is exceptionally cheap for LDS members; non-member tuition still beats most privates
Scholarships can be revoked for honor code violations including missionary misconduct
National Merit Finalists can be shut out if they don't first receive Presidential or Heritage awards
key_quotes: BYU has a great ranking in the accounting major and is cheap but I am a non-Mormon and an international student.
Members of the LDS church who resign membership no longer qualify for member tuition rates.
I got into BYU and got a $6272 scholarship AND the cougar pell promise, essentially giving me a full ride.

key_points: Academics are genuinely rigorous — transfer students frequently report grade shock
CS, accounting, and business programs are nationally ranked and well-regarded
syllabus.byu.edu is a student-discovered resource to scope workload before registering
key_quotes: When I was in Nevada, I was a 4.0 student and got an A in every economics class. Now I'm lucky to be passing.
I focused entirely on grades and socialized very little. I graduated in computer science with a 3.9 GPA, but finding a job was brutal without the network.
I cannot stress the importance of networking while at college.

key_points: Ecclesiastical endorsement required every semester — bishop ghosting can freeze enrollment
BYU mistakenly sent acceptance letters to 9 students, then initially reversed them
SOAR multicultural recruitment program quietly discontinued under new administration
key_quotes: Both my roommate and I have tried scheduling our ecclesiastical endorsement interviews with our bishop but he won't answer texts/calls. I can't enroll in classes because of the hold on my account.
One of these 9 is my nephew. It has truly wrecked him. He told friends. He told his family.
Heard from an insider that BYU might shut down the MSS office next year.

key_points: Campus is beautiful and highly walkable; a car is not necessary and parking actively sucks
Free public transit with BYU ID makes Provo surprisingly accessible for a U.S. city
Spring and summer semesters are ghost towns — clubs and events nearly disappear
key_quotes: I'm a current BYU student without a car. It's actually kind of nice because parking here sucks. Everybody ends up with some kind of fine or gets their car towed.
Provo has a pretty robust public transportation system (for the states at least) and it's all free with BYU ID.
BYU is active for only six months out of the year.

key_points: BYU ranks 9th globally for producing billion-dollar startup founders
Accounting and finance programs have strong Big 4 and investment banking placement
Networking is weak spot — students who only grind grades report struggling to find jobs
key_quotes: Breaking news: research from Stanford finds BYU 9th for unicorn founders. Kudos to BYU.
I graduated in computer science with a 3.9 GPA, but finding a job was brutal without the network.
BYU is a target school for finance although I do not know why. Already in banking, would not be hard to break back in.

key_points: Forbes ranked BYU 3rd nationally for best value in 2018; consistently top-tier ROI
Member tuition is strikingly low; even non-member rates beat most comparable universities
Off-campus housing costs eaten up by predatory property managers who own nearly everything
key_quotes: At least tuition for BYU is super cheap.
BYU Pathways: a Bachelors degree can be achieved in 3 years for under $10K.
Redstone is notorious for not maintaining the property and generally squeezing as much money as possible from you.

key_points: Overwhelmingly friendly and clean campus; students are genuinely kind to outsiders
It's not as MAGA as liberals think, nor as 'woke' as conservatives fear — it's its own thing
Influencers routinely exploit campus to manufacture viral 'weird Mormon' content
key_quotes: BYU students are actually very nice and welcoming. You're not judged for not being Mormon and you aren't pressured.
Lots of influencers come here and place students in very uncomfortable situations to try and make a viral Mormon moment. It's an ongoing problem.
I think BYU is simultaneously not as MAGA as liberals think it is, and not as 'woke' as MAGA thinks it is.

key_points: LGBTQ+: honor code can effectively expel students for identity, not just behavior
Athletes: two separate rape/assault cases involving BYU athletes in recent years
Housing: Redstone erases negative Google reviews and charges for everything
Administration: ecclesiastical endorsement system gives bishops power to freeze enrollment
key_quotes: BYU punishes belief/non-belief. In other words, a university that punishes thoughtcrime.
The way it's looking, this seems to be a lot more definitive and serious than Retzlaff's situation. Gross that this happened a year ago and he's still playing.
I came out as gay while at Mormon school, BYU. Dropped out after a year of horrible grades, problems with family.

key_points: syllabus.byu.edu lets you compare past workloads before registering — not widely known
Free NYT subscription and Xfinity Stream with HBO included for all students
No-car lifestyle is actually easier than having one — parking fines and towing are constant
key_quotes: syllabus.byu.edu saved me so much homework because I've chosen teachers with less projects.
Anyone know of any stuff you can get for free as a BYU student? I know about the NYT subscription and Xfinity Stream (and HBO Max through Xfinity).
Life with a car here kinda sucks because parking is awful.
